questions.q: What is the characteristic feature of amorphous solids?
questions.answer: Amorphous solids do not have a regular arrangement of their constituent particles.

questions.q: An element 'X' is present in two forms, i.e., cubic and monoclinic. When 'X' is in its cubic form, its density is 7.2 g cm-3 and in monoclinic form, it is 6.5 g cm-3. What can be concluded about the allotropes of 'X'?
questions.answer: Since the density of cubic and monoclinic forms of element 'X' are different, it indicates that they are allotropes.

questions.q: If the edge length of a cubic unit cell of 'Fe' is 388 pm, what is the density of the metal?
questions.answer: First, we need to calculate the volume of the unit cell. V = (edge length)^3. Then we can calculate the density using the formula: density = (mass of unit cell)/(volume of unit cell). The mass of the unit cell can be calculated using the atomic mass of 'Fe'.

questions.q: How do ionic solids differ from molecular solids?
questions.answer: Ionic solids are composed of ions arranged in a regular three-dimensional pattern, whereas molecular solids are composed of discrete molecules.

questions.q: What is the difference between the terms 'amorphous solid' and 'crystal'?
questions.answer: An amorphous solid lacks a regular arrangement of its particles, whereas a crystal has a regular arrangement of its constituent particles.

questions.q: What is meant by the term 'anisotropy'?
questions.answer: The property of a substance that varies in different directions.

questions.q: What is the characteristic feature of ionic solids?
What is the main difference between the two types of crystal lattices i.e., face-centered cubic lattice and body-centered cubic lattice?
questions.answer: The main difference between the two types of crystal lattices is the arrangement of atoms within the unit cell. In a face-centered cubic lattice, there is an atom at the center of each face of the cube, whereas in a body-centered cubic lattice, there is an atom at the center of the cube.

questions.q: An element 'A' crystallizes in a bcc (body-centered cubic) structure with a cell edge of 288 pm. What is the density of 'A'?
questions.answer: To calculate the density of 'A', we need to calculate the volume of the unit cell. V = (edge length)^3. Then we can calculate the density using the formula: density = (mass of unit cell)/(volume of unit cell). The mass of the unit cell can be calculated using the atomic mass of 'A'.

questions.q: What are allotropes? Give an example.
questions.answer: Allotropes are the different physical forms of the same element. An example is carbon which exists in the forms of diamond and graphite.
